$comment_socket_constraint: IMPORTANT: Socket.IO requires a persistent TCP connection and cannot run in
Vercel's serverless/edge functions. The Socket.IO server (server.ts) MUST be
deployed separately on a platform that supports long-lived Node.js processes,
such as Railway, Fly.io, Render, or DigitalOcean App Platform.
Set the SOCKET_SERVER_URL environment variable in your Vercel project settings
to point to the deployed socket server (e.g. https://bookex-socket.railway.app).
The NEXT_PUBLIC_SOCKET_URL variable used by the frontend client should match.

rewrites.$comment: Proxy /socket.io/* requests to the externally deployed Socket.IO server.

env.$comment: Set these in the Vercel dashboard under Project → Settings → Environment Variables.
